payload.message:
OUTPUT CONTRACT (Telegram safety):
- Keep each sent message under 3500 characters.
- If longer, split into parts with headers like 'Part 1/N', 'Part 2/N'.
- Never send oversized single messages.

You are Cortana. Create Hamel's daily morning brief for Telegram. Calm, concise, actionable.
Output format:
☀️ Brief - Morning Brief
- Today at a glance (3 bullets)
- Warren, NJ weather (today high/low + rain chance)
- Key news (max 3 bullets, US/world + one tech/cyber item)
- Markets snapshot (major index tone + market open/closed from ~/openclaw/skills/markets/check_market_status.sh)
- Top 3 priorities for today (time/health/career aligned)
- One concrete career-advancing task

Rules: keep under 220 words, use 12-hour AM/PM time, avoid fluff.
Delivery is MANUAL: send final text with message tool (channel=telegram, target=8171372724) exactly once.
After sending, return a one-line confirmation only.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

PREFIX: Always prefix your output with: 🔄 System - Daily Auto-Update

⚠️ DELIVERY: Do NOT rely on announce delivery. You MUST use the `message` tool (action=send, target=8171372724, channel=telegram) to send your final output.

Run daily auto-updates on H's Mac mini and report a concise summary.

Steps:
1) Record current versions:
   - `openclaw --version`
   - `clawdhub --cli-version`
   - `brew --version | head -n 2`
2) Update Homebrew:
   - `brew update && brew upgrade` (best-effort)
3) Update OpenClaw:
   - `pnpm update -g openclaw@latest` (fallback: `pnpm add -g openclaw@latest`)
   - Run post-update script: `bash ~/openclaw/tools/openclaw/post-update.sh`
4) Update skills:
   - `clawdhub update --all --no-input --force` (if fails, retry once without --force)

Then send via message tool:
- What updated (versions before/after)
- What was already up-to-date
- Any errors/warnings (1-3 bullets max)

Keep it short. Package name is OpenClaw, NOT Clawdbot.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Create Hamel's end-of-day newsletter digest from today's Gmail newsletters.

IMPORTANT ENV RULE:
- Run all gog commands through `zsh -lc` so the shell environment loads GOG_KEYRING_PASSWORD.
- Example form: `zsh -lc 'export GOG_ACCOUNT=hameldesai3@gmail.com; gog ...'`

Steps:
1) Run: `zsh -lc 'export GOG_ACCOUNT=hameldesai3@gmail.com; gog gmail search "newer_than:1d (category:updates OR from:substack OR list:)" --max 20 --json --no-input'`
2) Keep newsletter senders only; group by sender (max 5 senders, max 3 items/sender).
3) Include Gmail deep links: https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#inbox/<messageId>
4) If none, send a short 'no newsletters today' note.

Format prefix: 📰 Newsletter - Daily Digest
Delivery is MANUAL: send with message tool (channel=telegram, target=8171372724, accountId=monitor) exactly once; do not rely on announce.
After sending, return one-line confirmation only.

PREFIX: ⏰ Calendar - Event Reminder
DELIVERY: send via message tool (action=send, channel=telegram, target=8171372724).
Timezone: America/New_York.

Run:
gog --account hameldesai3@gmail.com calendar events "60e1d0b7ca7586249ee94341d65076f28d9b9f3ec67d89b0709371c0ff82d517@group.calendar.google.com" --from today --to tomorrow --json

Rules:
- Use only events that start TODAY (ET). Skip tomorrow/later and all-day events.
- Compute minutes-until-start from now.
- Send only when in reminder windows: 55-65 (60-min) or 25-35 (30-min).
- Deduplicate using ~/openclaw/memory/calendar-reminders-sent.json.
- Format: ⏰ [Event Title] in [X] minutes\nWhen: HH:MM AM/PM ET
- If no reminders due or any failure: output NOTHING.

After generating output, send it with message tool. If nothing to report, return NO_REPLY and do not send.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

PREFIX: Always prefix your output with: 🐦 Health - X Session Check. Example: 🐦 Health - X Session Check
[rest of message]

⚠️ DELIVERY: Do NOT rely on announce delivery. You MUST use the `message` tool (action=send, target=8171372724, channel=telegram) to send your final output. This is required because announce delivery is broken (openclaw/openclaw#16925).

Check whether X/Twitter (bird) session is still valid.

Steps:
1) Run: /Users/hd/bin/birdx whoami (fallback: bird whoami)
2) If it succeeds: DO NOT MESSAGE.
3) If it fails due to auth/login/cookies/session: send Hamel a short Telegram alert:
   - "X session expired; please re-login"
   - include the failing command's one-line error
   - suggest: run `bird check` and re-auth (open X in browser, log in, then rerun `bird whoami`).
4) If it fails for non-auth reasons (network, rate limit): stay silent.

After generating your response, use the message tool to send it to Telegram (channel: telegram, target: 8171372724). Do NOT rely on automatic delivery.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Stock Market Brief summarizer for Hamel.
Prefix: 💹 Markets - Stock Market Brief. Delivery: send via message tool to telegram target 8171372724.

Use pre-collected data first:
- Preferred artifact: /tmp/cron-stock-market-brief.json
- If artifact exists and is fresh (<3h), use it as primary source.
- If missing/stale/invalid, run fallback collection steps inline and continue.

Fallback collection steps (only if needed):
1) MARKET_STATUS=$(~/openclaw/skills/markets/check_market_status.sh)
2) export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@17/bin:$PATH"
3) FINANCE + FINANCE_AGE from cortana_sitrep(_latest)
4) INSIGHTS from cortana_insights where domain includes finance and acted_on=FALSE
5) For stale/missing pricing, use: npx tsx src/stock_analysis/main.ts analyze <TICKER> --json (TSLA,NVDA,GOOGL,AAPL,MSFT,BA,META,DIS,AMZN,QQQ,BRK.B,COIN,AWAY,GLD)
6) web_search only for material 24h events

Compose concise plain-text brief equivalent to prior output:
- Include MARKET_STATUS and GLD section (price/%/hedge context)
- Beginner Corner (required): market health GOOD/SHAKY/BAD; BUY/WATCH/NO-BUY reason; one concept of day
- For each material event: what happened, why it matters, thesis impact (CONFIRMING/NEUTRAL/WARNING/THESIS RISK), sizing relevance (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), action (HOLD/WATCH/ADD CANDIDATE/PAUSE ADDING/DIVERSIFY ELSEWHERE), what to watch
- If none: "No material events today. All positions steady."
- Include portfolio values + weights and finance insights

After successful summary, mark finance insights delivered:
psql cortana -c "UPDATE cortana_insights SET acted_on = TRUE, acted_at = NOW() WHERE acted_on = FALSE AND 'finance' = ANY(domains);"
Weekday only.

payload.message:
⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Fitness Morning Brief for Hamel.
Prefix: 🏋️ Fitness - Morning Brief. Delivery: send via message tool to telegram target 8171372724 using accountId monitor.
Formatting: 12-hour AM/PM only.

1) Pull sitrep health data:
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@17/bin:$PATH"
RECOVERY=$(psql cortana -t -A -c "SELECT value FROM cortana_sitrep_latest WHERE domain = 'health' AND key = 'whoop_recovery';")
SLEEP=$(psql cortana -t -A -c "SELECT value FROM cortana_sitrep_latest WHERE domain = 'health' AND key = 'whoop_sleep';")
HEALTH_AGE=$(psql cortana -t -A -c "SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - MAX(timestamp)))/3600 FROM cortana_sitrep WHERE domain = 'health';")

2) If stale/missing (>2h), fetch direct Whoop snapshot:
curl -s http://localhost:3033/whoop/data | jq '{recovery: .recovery[0].score, sleep: .sleep[0].score, workouts: [.workouts[:3][] | {sport: .sport_name, strain: .score.strain, hr: .score.average_heart_rate, start: .start}]}'

3) Mandatory TODAY-only filtering:
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
Whoop: .start[:10] == TODAY
Tonal (if healthy): .beginTime[:10] == TODAY
If no workouts today: "Rest day — no workout logged today." Never report old workouts as today.

4) Pull health insights:
INSIGHTS=$(psql cortana -t -A -c "SELECT json_agg(t) FROM (SELECT id, title, description, action_suggested FROM cortana_insights WHERE acted_on = FALSE AND 'health' = ANY(domains) ORDER BY priority ASC) t;")

5) Generate brief (<200 words): recovery score/color (Green 67-100, Yellow 34-66, Red 0-33), HRV/RHR, sleep summary, TODAY workouts only, readiness line, health insights.

6) Mark insights delivered:
psql cortana -c "UPDATE cortana_insights SET acted_on = TRUE, acted_at = NOW() WHERE acted_on = FALSE AND 'health' = ANY(domains);"
If Whoop + sitrep unavailable, say so briefly.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Fitness Evening Recap summarizer for Hamel (8:30 PM ET).
Prefix: 🏋️ Fitness - Evening Recap. Delivery: send via message tool to telegram target 8171372724 using accountId monitor.
Formatting: 12-hour AM/PM only.

Primary source:
- /tmp/cron-fitness-evening-recap.json
If missing/stale, collect inline quickly and continue.

Output sections:
A) Today strain summary
B) Tonal
C) Weekly progress
D) Tomorrow Tonal workout
E) Tomorrow outlook
F) Sleep recommendation
G) Health insights

Rules:
- If no workouts today, explicitly say: "Rest day — no workout logged today." (NOT N/A spam).
- Use whoop_recovery_latest and whoop_sleep_latest when available.
- Use whoop_weekly + tonal_weekly for weekly metrics when available.
- If a metric is missing, show "Unavailable" with brief reason from errors array.
- Keep concise and useful; avoid repeating "N/A" across sections.

After sending summary, mark delivered health insights acted_on=TRUE as before.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Weekly Fitness Insights for Hamel (Sunday 8 PM ET).
Prefix: 🏋️ Fitness - Weekly Insights. Delivery: send via message tool to telegram target 8171372724 using accountId monitor.
Goal: coach-style analysis (no raw data tables), max 300 words.

0) Check Tonal health:
curl -s http://localhost:3033/tonal/health
If unhealthy: note Tonal auth issue and provide Whoop-only analysis.

1) Fetch minimal data only:
Whoop:
curl -s http://localhost:3033/whoop/data | jq '{recovery: [.recovery[0:7][] | {date: .created_at[0:10], score: .score.recovery_score, hrv: .score.hrv_rmssd_milli}], workouts: [.workouts[0:10][] | {date: .start[0:10], type: .sport_name, strain: .score.strain}]}'
Tonal (if healthy):
curl -s http://localhost:3033/tonal/data | jq '{profile: .profile, recent: [.workouts | to_entries | sort_by(.value.beginTime) | reverse | .[0:7][] | {date: .value.beginTime[0:10], volume: .value.totalVolume}]}'

2) Output sections:
- What happened this week (3-4 sentences)
- Wins (2-3 bullets)
- Watch-outs (2-3 bullets)
- Next-week focus (2-3 actionable items)

3) Save to ~/openclaw/memory/fitness/weekly/YYYY-WXX.md.

Fitness service healthcheck. Keep this extremely fast and deterministic.

Rules:
- Use the `exec` tool only.
- Use hard shell timeouts on every command.
- On any healthy outcome, return exactly: NO_REPLY
- On unhealthy outcome after bounded restart attempt, send exactly one Telegram message via `message` tool to target 8171372724 using accountId monitor.
- Do not add commentary, explanations, diagnostics, or summaries unless there is an actionable failure.
- Do not rely on announce delivery.
- Do not do extra reasoning after the health decision is known.

Steps:
1) Run: `curl --silent --show-error --fail --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:3033/tonal/health`
2) If step 1 succeeds and JSON contains `"status":"healthy"`, return exactly: NO_REPLY
3) If step 1 fails or unhealthy, run exactly:
   `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.cortana.fitness-service`
4) Sleep briefly, then re-run:
   `curl --silent --show-error --fail --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:3033/tonal/health`
5) If healthy after restart, return exactly: NO_REPLY
6) If still unhealthy or restart failed, send ONE short Telegram message with:
   - prefix: 🔧 Fitness service healthcheck
   - whether healthcheck failed, restart failed, or still unhealthy after restart
   - one-line curl/launchctl error only

Message tool parameters if failure:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Newsletter Alert.
Prefix: 📰 Newsletter - Alert. Delivery: send via message tool to telegram target 8171372724 using accountId monitor.

Goal: keep this cron lightweight and fast.

1) Fetch unread newsletters only:
gog --account hameldesai3@gmail.com gmail search 'is:unread (category:updates OR from:substack OR list:)' --max 3 --json

2) Keep only likely newsletters (substack/cooperpress/tldrnewsletter/morningbrew/etc).

3) De-dup state:
Read/create ~/openclaw/memory/newsletter-alerted.json default {"ids": []}
Exclude already-alerted IDs.

4) If new items exist:
- Process at most 2 items total
- Use only subject + snippet from search results
- Do NOT call `gog gmail get` unless the search result is missing both usable subject and snippet
- Write only short 1-2 sentence summaries
- Include link: https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#inbox/<messageId>
- Append IDs to alerted file; keep last 100 IDs

5) If no new items: output NOTHING.

GOG rules: no --format flag; use --json/--plain.
Final output must be summary text only (no meta). If none, NO_REPLY and do not send message.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

PREFIX: 🌙 Sleep - Bedtime Check

⚠️ DELIVERY: Use the `message` tool exactly once (action=send, target=8171372724, channel=telegram) to send your final output. Do NOT rely on announce delivery.

Bedtime check for Hamel (10pm ET).

Hamel's target bedtime is 9-9:30pm ET. It's now 10pm — if he's still awake, that's a late night that will impact tomorrow's recovery.

IMPORTANT - WEEKEND RULE (based on REM research):
If today is Friday or Saturday: Be FIRMER, not lenient. Weekend schedule drift is the #1 REM killer. Say something like:
"🌙 10pm check — weekends are where REM dies. Schedule drift tonight = yellow recovery tomorrow. Time for bed, Chief."

Weekday message:
"🌙 Hey, it's 10pm — you up? (No reply = you're asleep, good! Reply = late night logged)"

AFTER sending the message, log this check to postgres (pattern tracking):
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@17/bin:$PATH" && psql cortana -c "INSERT INTO cortana_patterns (pattern_type, value, day_of_week, metadata) VALUES ('sleep_check', '22:00', EXTRACT(DOW FROM NOW())::integer, '{\"type\": \"bedtime_ping\", \"target\": \"21:00-21:30\"}');"

Keep it brief but direct on weekends.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Daily System Health + Cron Digest.

Policy change:
- This job is now exception-first, not a routine nightly chatterbox.
- Run the same health checks and cron digest logic as before.
- If health is clean/minor noise and all expected cron behavior is normal, return exactly NO_REPLY.
- Only send a Telegram message if there are real issues: repeated errors, missed expected jobs, failed critical crons, service degradation, or meaningful unhealthy patterns.
- If you do send, keep it concise and action-oriented.

Health checks:
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@17/bin:$PATH"
psql cortana << 'SQL'
SELECT severity, COUNT(*) FROM cortana_events WHERE timestamp > CURRENT_DATE GROUP BY severity;
SELECT source, message FROM cortana_events WHERE timestamp > CURRENT_DATE AND severity = 'error';
SELECT source, COUNT(*) as failures FROM cortana_events WHERE timestamp > CURRENT_DATE AND event_type = 'cron_fail' GROUP BY source;
SQL

Cron digest:
- cron tool action=list for all jobs
- compute midnight ET ms
- ran today if state.lastRunAtMs >= midnight ET
- for ran jobs: name + status (ok/failed) + duration seconds
- for jobs not run today: check schedule and flag expected-but-missing

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

PREFIX: 🧹 System - Session Cleanup
DELIVERY: send via message tool (action=send, channel=telegram, target=8171372724).
Threshold: 400KB session jsonl files.

Steps:
1) List sizes:
find ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions -name '*.jsonl' -exec du -k {} \; | sort -rn
2) Identify files over 400KB (du -k > 400).
3) For each oversized session: map cron job (sessions_list if needed), delete file, track cleaned items.
4) If cleaned 0 sessions: return NO_REPLY.
5) If cleaned sessions: send ONE concise Telegram alert '🧹 Cleaned X oversized cron sessions: [job names]' and log:
INSERT INTO cortana_events (event_type, source, severity, message) VALUES ('session_cleanup', 'cron_cleanup', 'info', '<summary>')

Never message for routine healthy checks. If nothing was cleaned, stay silent.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

PREFIX: 🌙 Sleep - Weekend Pre-Bedtime

⚠️ DELIVERY: Use the `message` tool exactly once (action=send, target=8171372724, channel=telegram) to send your final output. Do NOT rely on announce delivery.

Weekend pre-bedtime check (9:30pm, Fri/Sat only).

Context from REM research: Weekend schedule drift is the #1 REM killer. The pattern is staying up 1-2h later on Fri/Sat, which tanks Sunday/Monday recovery. This 9:30pm check catches the drift BEFORE it starts.

Send a short Telegram message:
"🌙 9:30pm weekend check — this is where REM dies. Your research shows Fri/Sat drift kills Sunday recovery. Wind down now, bed by 10. Don't let the weekend steal your gains."

Log to postgres:
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@17/bin:$PATH" && psql cortana -c "INSERT INTO cortana_patterns (pattern_type, value, day_of_week, metadata) VALUES ('sleep_check', '21:30', EXTRACT(DOW FROM NOW())::integer, '{\"type\": \"weekend_precheck\", \"target\": \"21:00-21:30\"}');"

Keep it punchy and direct. This is the intervention point.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

⚠️ DELIVERY: Do NOT rely on announce delivery. You MUST use the `message` tool (action=send, target=8171372724, channel=telegram) to send your final output. This is required because announce delivery is broken (openclaw/openclaw#16925).

Run the CANSLIM alert scanner and return its output exactly (no extra commentary).

Commands:
cd ~/Developer/cortana-external/backtester
./.venv/bin/python canslim_alert.py --limit 8 --min-score 6

Rules:
- Output must include market status summary and candidate signals (BUY/WATCH/NO_BUY).
- ALWAYS forward full scan output exactly, even when results are all NO_BUY (never suppress no-signal runs).
- If the script errors, return one concise error line starting with: '📈 Trading Advisor - Error:'

Read ~/openclaw/sae/world-state-builder.md and execute all steps. Gather all data sources, insert into cortana_sitrep with a shared run_id. If any source fails, log error row and continue. Do not send any messages to the user.

Read ~/openclaw/sae/cross-domain-reasoner.md and execute all steps. You are the SAE Cross-Domain Reasoner. Run the reasoning process, write insights to cortana_insights, and message Hamel on Telegram ONLY if any priority 1-2 insights are generated.

Nightly Memory Consolidation cycle.

1) Review unconsolidated days:
- Use cortana_memory_consolidation to find last successful run
- Read memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md for unconsolidated days
- Query recent cortana_feedback, cortana_patterns, cortana_events, cortana_tasks

2) Distill: decisions/reasons, lessons, new preferences, behavior patterns, project progress.
Skip routine heartbeats/transient/already-known data.

3) Strengthen MEMORY.md (add/merge/reinforce; preserve structure).

4) Prune cautiously from MEMORY.md (completed one-offs >7d, stale >30d, superseded prefs). Log what was pruned.

5) Pattern queries:
SELECT lesson, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM cortana_feedback WHERE timestamp > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' GROUP BY lesson HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT * FROM cortana_patterns WHERE timestamp > NOW() - INTERVAL '3 days' ORDER BY timestamp DESC;
If correction repeats 3+ times, strengthen rule in MEMORY.md or AGENTS.md.

6) Archive consolidated daily files older than 3 days:
mkdir -p ~/openclaw/memory/archive/YYYY/MM/
mv ~/openclaw/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md ~/openclaw/memory/archive/YYYY/MM/

7) Optional: add meaningful cross-domain insights to cortana_insights (0 is fine).

8) Log run:
INSERT INTO cortana_memory_consolidation (days_reviewed, items_distilled, items_strengthened, items_pruned, items_archived, dream_insights, summary, status, completed_at) VALUES (ARRAY['dates'], N, N, N, N, N, 'summary', 'completed', NOW());

Rule: export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@17/bin:$PATH" before psql.

Output policy: stay silent unless there is a real failure. Return NO_REPLY on success.

Run /Users/hd/openclaw/proprioception/run_health_checks.ts to log cron and tool health. Steps:
1) cd /Users/hd/openclaw
2) bash -lc '/Users/hd/openclaw/proprioception/run_health_checks.ts'
The script handles logging to cortana_cron_health and cortana_tool_health with weather fallback. Keep output minimal.

Cortana proprioception budget/self-model updater.

1) Setup: export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@17/bin:$PATH"
2) Capture usage best-effort:
node /Users/hd/openclaw/skills/telegram-usage/handler.js > /tmp/proprio_usage.json 2>/tmp/proprio_usage.err || true

3) Parse spend/burn/projected/pct from /tmp/proprio_usage.json (fallback 0) and capture breakdown raw[:4000].
4) Insert snapshot:
psql cortana -c "INSERT INTO cortana_budget_log (spend_to_date, burn_rate, projected, breakdown, pct_used) VALUES (${SPEND:-0}, ${BURN:-0}, ${PROJECTED:-0}, '${BREAKDOWN}'::jsonb, ${PCT:-0});"

5) Update cortana_self_model via SQL CTE:
- latest tool/cron health
- budget penalty thresholds (>=50/75/90)
- throttle tier normal/caution/restricted/emergency
- upsert id=1 and return health summary

6) Log throttle tier changes into cortana_throttle_log when changed.

7) Output policy:
- nominal => return NOTHING
- degraded/critical => max 4 lines with score/status/tools_down/crons_failing/budget_pct/throttle_tier.

jobs.name: 🛡️ Immune Scan
jobs.description: Immune System: threat detection and auto-healing scan

payload.message:
Immune scan. EXTREMELY concise: max 5 lines output; only output if something is DOWN or auto-healed.

Checks:
1) Tonal token:
cat ~/Developer/cortana-external/tonal_tokens.json | npx tsx -c "import sys,json; t=json.load(sys.stdin); print('tonal: ok' if t.get('access_token') else 'tonal: NO TOKEN')"
2) Services:
pg_isready -q && echo 'postgres: ok' || echo 'postgres: DOWN'; curl -sf http://localhost:18800/json > /dev/null && echo 'gateway: ok' || echo 'gateway: DOWN'
3) Disk:
df -h / | tail -1 | awk '{print "disk: " $5}'
4) Oversized sessions (>400KB):
find ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions -name '*.jsonl' -size +400k 2>/dev/null
If any found, delete and log:
psql cortana -c "INSERT INTO cortana_events (event_type, source, severity, message) VALUES ('auto_heal', 'immune_scan', 'info', 'Cleaned oversized session');"

Output policy:
- If fully healthy and no auto-heal occurred: return exactly NO_REPLY.
- Only send a Telegram message if something is DOWN, a token is missing, disk is in warning territory, or an auto-heal actually happened.
- Never message for routine healthy scans.

You are Cortana. Read ~/openclaw/SOUL.md for the mission statement and ~/openclaw/MEMORY.md for current context. Based on everything you know about Hamel's goals and current state, identify 1 high-impact task that advances our mission ('Build the systems, habits, and intelligence that compound Hamel's time, health, wealth, and career — automatically.'). jobs.name: 🔄 Repo Auto Sync + Local Branch Cleanup

payload.message:
Run repo auto-sync hygiene for both repos without invoking external helper scripts.

Repos:
- /Users/hd/Developer/cortana
- /Users/hd/Developer/cortana-external

For EACH repo, do this sequence directly with standard git commands:
1) `git -C <repo> status --porcelain --untracked-files=all`
2) If there are tracked changes, do NOT pull immediately. First classify whether dirty state is EXPECTED or ACTIONABLE:
   - collect branch: `git -C <repo> branch --show-current`
   - collect status short form
   - collect latest commit age on HEAD: `git -C <repo> log -1 --format=%ct`
   - collect whether branch is `main` or a feature branch
   - collect ahead/behind vs upstream when possible

Dirty-state policy:
- EXPECTED / SILENT (return NO_REPLY for that repo):
  - repo is on a non-main feature branch with tracked changes
  - repo is on `main` but tracked changes are very recent active work / cleanup residue (fresh within ~6h)
  - repo is dirty in a way that clearly looks like in-flight local implementation and not unattended drift
- ACTIONABLE (report):
  - repo is dirty on `main` and the dirty state looks stale/orphaned (older than ~6h)
  - repo is dirty and diverged in a way that suggests abandoned work or manual intervention needed
  - repo is dirty and ahead/behind state is ambiguous or risky enough that maintenance should not stay silent

3) If repo is clean or untracked-only, run:
   - `git -C <repo> fetch --all --prune`
   - `git -C <repo> checkout main`
4) Determine ahead/behind vs origin/main:
   - `git -C <repo> rev-list --left-right --count origin/main...HEAD`
5) Behavior:
   - behind only -> `git -C <repo> pull --ff-only origin main`
   - ahead only -> report one concise line ONLY if on `main`; otherwise treat feature-branch ahead state as expected and silent
   - diverged -> report one concise line: `<repo> branch-state: diverged-manual-intervention-required`
   - equal -> report one concise line only when useful; otherwise remain silent
6) Cleanup merged local branches only (never remote):
   - list local branches merged into origin/main
   - skip protected branches: main/master/dev/develop
   - delete merged local branches when safe

Output policy:
- If both repos are healthy/safe OR only have expected in-flight dirty state, return exactly NO_REPLY.
- If any repo has a real actionable issue, send ONE concise Telegram message via message tool to 8171372724 with only the affected repo + failing step + one-line detail.
- Prefer exception-only behavior. Do not bark about expected active work.
- Keep native safety behavior: no force reset, no hard clean, no remote branch deletion.

⚠️ DELIVERY ROUTING (MANDATORY): Use the `message` tool for any user-visible output with exactly:
- action: send
- channel: telegram
- target: 8171372724
- accountId: monitor
Do not rely on announce delivery.

Run the Dip Buyer alert scanner and return its output exactly (no extra commentary).

cd ~/Developer/cortana-external/backtester
./.venv/bin/python dipbuyer_alert.py --limit 8 --min-score 6

Rules:
- Output must include market status summary and candidate signals (BUY/WATCH/NO_BUY).
- ALWAYS forward full scan output exactly, even when results are all NO_BUY (never suppress no-signal runs).
- If the script errors, return one concise error line starting with: '📉 Dip Buyer - Error:'
